# EKS Token Format

> **Status**: Architecture fully understood (2026-04-07). Plaintext contents are server-side only
> and intentionally opaque to the client. No further reverse engineering is needed or possible
> without the server key.

## What Is eks

`eks` is the `info` field returned by `TDC.getInfo()` in the Tencent ChaosVM runtime.
It is sent as the `eks` field in the `/cap_union_new_verify` POST body alongside `collect`.

## Architecture: Server-Baked, Not VM-Generated

**eks is not generated by the VM at runtime.** It is baked into every `tdc.js` response
by Tencent's CDN/backend server at script-serve time — before the bytecode VM even starts.

```
tdc.js structure (first ~130 lines, before VM):

  Line 1:   window.TDC_NAME = "FgTaXfOKnXnnZNVNAFlgbmQWHJNVaSBk";
  ...
  Line 123: window.FgTaXfOKnXnnZNVNAFlgbmQWHJNVaSBk = '<base64 ciphertext>';
  Line 124: var __TENCENT_CHAOS_VM = function() { ...   ← VM starts here
```

The VM only *reads* the value — it never generates or transforms it.

## Verified Facts

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Length | 312 chars (base64) |
| Decoded length | 232 bytes = 29 × 8 (exact XTEA block boundary) |
| Base64 variant | Standard (`+` and `/`), not URL-safe |
| Padding | `==` (2 chars) |
| Segment layout | Single segment — no 4-segment assembly |
| Source | Pre-baked in tdc.js by Tencent server |
| POST value | Identical to baked value — VM passes through unchanged |
| Encryption | XTEA (assumed), server-side key — unknown to client |
| Per-build | Different value per tdc.js response, even with same TDC_NAME |

## The VM Read Chain (Pass-Through Only)

```
[Tencent server]
  generate eks plaintext (session metadata, appid, nonce, timestamp, ...)
  XTEA-encrypt with server-side key
  btoa (standard base64) → 312-char string
  embed at tdc.js line 123 as:
    window["<TDC_NAME>"] = '<312-char ciphertext>';

[Browser loads tdc.js]
  window["FgTa..."] = baked value   ← already set before VM starts
  ↓
  VM func_53 (TDC init, PC 11540):
    PC 11680: PROP_GET r29 = window["FgTa..."]   ← READ ONLY
    PC 11684: r42["info"] = r29
    PC 11688: r39[0] = {info: eks_value}
    PC 11755: FUNC_CREATE_C → func_25 closure captures [r15, r39]
  ↓
  TDC.getInfo() → func_25 (entryPC=6436)
    returns r39[0] = {info: eks_value}   ← unchanged
  ↓
  POST body: eks = info value   ← unchanged, sent verbatim
```

## What eks Proves (Security Model)

`collect` proves "this browser is real" (client-built fingerprints).
`eks` proves "this script came from our server" (server-issued authenticity token).

```
Browser                    Tencent CDN                 Tencent Verify Backend
   │── GET /tdc.js ──────>│                                      │
   │                       │  generate eks:                       │
   │                       │    {appid, nonce, token, iat, ...}   │
   │                       │    XTEA-encrypt (server key)         │
   │<── tdc.js (eks baked) │                                      │
   │                       │                                      │
   │  [VM runs, collects browser fingerprints → collect token]    │
   │                       │                                      │
   │── POST /verify ───────────────────────────────────────────>  │
   │    collect = client-built fingerprints                       │
   │    eks     = server-issued blob, unchanged                   │
   │                       │   decrypt eks (server key)           │
   │                       │   verify: nonce matches, ts fresh,   │
   │                       │   appid correct                      │
```

## Why eks Cannot Be Forged

| Attack | collect alone | collect + eks |
|--------|--------------|---------------|
| Replay a captured collect token | ✅ might work | ❌ eks timestamp/nonce is stale |
| Bot farm generating collect tokens | ✅ might pass | ❌ eks requires a real tdc.js fetch |
| Forge collect without fetching tdc.js | ✅ possible | ❌ can't forge eks (no server key) |

## Implication for Reimplementation

**You do not need to reverse the eks encryption.**

eks is a free ticket included with every `tdc.js` fetch. The correct flow is:

```
1. GET https://captcha.tencentcloudapi.com/tdc.js?appid=...
2. Extract eks from the response text:
     const eks = tdcJs.match(/window\.[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,}\s*=\s*'([^']+)'/)[1];
   (or use TDC.getInfo().info after loading the script)
3. Run collect reimplementation (token/ directory)
4. POST /verify with both collect and eks
```

`puppeteer/collect-generator.js` already does this correctly — it loads tdc.js and
calls `TDC.getInfo().info` to retrieve the pre-baked eks value.
